title: Drawing the Lines
points: Redrawing boundaries of assembly & Lok Sabha constituencies.
Done by the Delimitation Commission.
Ensures equal representation based on population changes.

quiz.question: Does Delimitation happen before every single election?
options: No, it is done periodically by a commission

title: Getting on the Roll
points: Revision of Electoral Rolls happens 6 months prior.
Additions (18+ turning eligible) and deletions (deceased).
Forms 6 (Registration) and Form 8 (Correction) are used.

quiz.question: Which form should a new voter fill out?

title: Sounding the Bugle
points: ECI Press Conference announcing dates.
Model Code of Conduct (MCC) comes into effect immediately.
Lays down rules for governments, candidates, and parties.

quiz.question: When does the MCC come into effect?

title: The Referee's Rulebook
points: Ensures level playing field during campaigning.
Governments cannot announce new schemes or projects.
Strict action against hate speech and bribing voters.

quiz.question: Can the ruling party announce a new freebie scheme during MCC?

title: Throwing the Hat in the Ring
points: Candidates file papers detailing assets and criminal records.
Returning Officer scrutinizes the papers for validity.
Candidates have a window to withdraw their nomination.

quiz.question: Do candidates have to declare their criminal records?

title: Winning Hearts & Minds
points: Rallies, manifestos, and door-to-door visits.
Strict expenditure limits are enforced by ECI.
Ends 48 hours before polling (The Silence Period).

quiz.question: What is the 'Silence Period'?
options: The 48 hours right before voting ends
A period where no one can speak to the media

title: The Power of Ink
points: Voters use EVMs to cast their vote.
VVPAT provides a physical paper trail for verification.
Inked finger is the ultimate symbol of democratic participation.

title: The Moment of Truth
points: Simultaneous counting for all phases happens on a set date.
EVMs are opened in the presence of candidate agents.
First-Past-The-Post system determines the winner.

quiz.question: In the First-Past-The-Post system, does a candidate need 50%+ votes to win?
options: No, just more than anyone else

title: A New Beginning
points: Returning Officer gives the winning certificate.
The party/coalition with a majority forms the government.
The democratic cycle begins anew.

quiz.question: Who gives the winning certificate to the candidate?
